Protein names: Ornithine lipid N-methyltransferase (OL N-methyltransferase) (EC 2.1.1.344) (Ornithine lipid synthesis G)
Organism: Singulisphaera acidiphila (strain ATCC BAA-1392 / DSM 18658 / VKM B-2454 / MOB10)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=an N(2)-[(3R)-3-(2-saturated-acyloxy)acyl]-L-ornithine lipid + 3 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = an N,N,N-trimethylornithine lipid + 3 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 3 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:20461, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:138473, ChEBI:CHEBI:138481; EC=2.1.1.344; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:25925947}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:20462; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:25925947};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the 3-fold methylation of ornithine lipids. Forms ornithine lipids that are mono-, di-, and trimethylated on the delta-nitrogen of the ornithine head group. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:25925947}.
